channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top Democrat on House Ethics panel says Gaetz report should be released
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (D-Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, said she thinks the panel’s report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) should be released.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Photo-shoot fail: Biden, Trudeau miss traditional photo with world leaders at G20
item.description: President Joe Biden inadvertently was a missing man in the traditional photo of world leaders at the final Group of 20 summit of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Transgender women to be banned from Capitol Hill female bathrooms under new House GOP proposal
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace is introducing a resolution to ban transgender women from female bathrooms at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems' Gaetz outrage follows long history of questionable DOJ acts under Biden
item.description: Criticisms have mounted that former Rep. Matt Gaetz as attorney general and would break norms, despite a list of questionable actions taken by DOJ under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania Supreme Court upholds exclusion of certain mail-in ballots, in victory for GOP
item.description: The Keystone State's top court reaffirmed that certain mail-in ballots cannot be counted, in a blow to certain Democrat-led counties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau says Canada 'could have acted quicker' on reining in immigration
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said the federal government in Canada "could have acted quicker" to reign in the massive influx of migrants entering the country following the coronavirus pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Chris Wright, Trump's choice to run the Energy Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Fossil fuel executive Chris Wright, Donald Trump's choice for Energy secretary, is a strong supporter of oil and gas development, including fracking, a key pillar of the president-elect’s quest for U.S. “energy dominance” in the global market.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden asks Congress for $98 billion in Helene, Milton disaster relief funding
item.description: President Biden is asking Congress for nearly $100 billion in disaster relief to deal with storms Milton and Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: All eyes on House Ethics Committee amid controversy over Gaetz report
item.description: All eyes will be locked on the House Ethics Committee this week as it weighs whether to release its report into Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied longer range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike deeper inside Russia, easing limitations on the longer range weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps FCC's Brendan Carr to lead the agency
item.description: Carr was seen as a pretty conventional Republican with a pro-corporate outlook for most of his career. More recently, he has embraced Trumpian themes about social media, tech and television companies.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Just before the election, a poll from Selzer sent shockwaves nationwide after it found Vice President Harris leading President-elect Trump in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg called to apologize for saying bakery denied order over politics
item.description: New York City politicians are calling on Whoopi Goldberg to apologize for suggesting on air that a local bakery had declined to make “The View” co-host a batch of desserts for her birthday because of her political beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Women stock up on abortion pills and Plan B, fearing new restrictions under Trump
item.description: Telehealth providers say requests for the pills have spiked since the election. Patients and doctors worry what a Trump presidency could mean for medical abortion and emergency contraception.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden and Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: What Trump's pledge to close Dept. of Education means for students, GOP-led states
item.description: President-elect Trump promised to close the Department of Education. We asked several education policy experts what the impacts of doing so would mean for students and the country.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep and Taylor Haney, MPR News

item.title: Satire slinger The Onion buys Alex Jones' Infowars at auction with help from Sandy Hook families
item.description: The satirical news publication The Onion won the bidding for Alex Jones’ Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, backed by families of Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting victims whom Jones owes more than $1 billion in defamation judgments for calling the massacre a hoax, the families announced Thursday
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jacky Rosen looks forward to 2nd term with focus on economy; Nevada senator strongly opposes mass deportation
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen plans to focus on the economy and kitchen-table issues after voters re-elected her to a second term in Congress, she told 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Handful of votes separate Las Vegas-area council candidates, echoing their primary
item.description: Just a handful of votes separated two candidates for a North Las Vegas City Council seat as of Tuesday -- a repeat from their June primary, according to the latest totals from Clark County.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tapping Noem to lead Homeland Security: Reports
item.description: Noem was first floated as a possible running mate for Trump, but her consideration fizzled after the release of her biography, in which she described killing a dog that was “untrainable.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9
